checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for ACTION-CHECK-INPUT-LENGTH: The secp256r1 precompile input must be exactly 160 bytes. Inputs of any other le

reachability.notes: Attacker can invoke precompile via on-chain transaction with arbitrary calldata length.

checklist_item.detection_procedure:
1. Identify the trust boundary at ACTION-CHECK-INPUT-LENGTH (trust level: UNTRUSTED, target: unknown).
2. Property: The secp256r1 precompile input must be exactly 160 bytes. Inputs of any other length must cause the precompile to return empty output without reverting.
3. Target interface: N/A.
4. Attack vectors: generic boundary fuzzing.
5. Craft adversarial inputs crossing this boundary to test validation completeness.
6. Verify all input validation, authentication, and data sanitization are enforced.
7. Check for bypass paths that skip validation.
8. Severity justification: Incorrect length handling could cause divergent behavior across clients, leading to consensus split affecting >33% of network.
executable_checks: Fuzz ACTION-CHECK-INPUT-LENGTH with malformed inputs crossing the trust boundary
Verify input validation completeness for unknown
Test boundary with maximum-size and minimum-size payloads
Check for consensus-splitting behavior across different client implementations
checklist_item.notes: Source property: PROP-W0-EIP7951-PRECOND-001. Boundary edge: ACTION-CHECK-INPUT-LENGTH. Trust level: UNTRUSTED. Target: unknown.

checklist_item.title: Falsify Pre-condition on ACTION-CHECK-GAS: The CLZ opcode must require exactly 5 gas. If less than 5 gas is avail

reachability.notes: Incorrect gas cost could enable DoS via underpriced computation.

checklist_item.detection_procedure:
1. Pre-condition: The CLZ opcode must require exactly 5 gas. If less than 5 gas is available, execution must fail with an out-of-gas error.
2. Enumerate inputs that should be rejected by this pre-condition.
3. Attempt bypass with:
   a. Invalid/malformed inputs that may slip through validation
   b. Edge case values (zero, max, negative, empty)
   c. Type confusion or encoding tricks
4. Verify correct rejection of all invalid inputs.
5. Check if bypassing leads to exploitable state.
6. Severity justification: Incorrect gas pricing could enable computation-heavy blocks but CLZ is inherently fast.
executable_checks: Attempt precondition bypass on ACTION-CHECK-GAS with crafted invalid inputs
Test edge-case values (zero, max, overflow) against precondition
Verify rejection behavior is consistent across all code paths
checklist_item.notes: Source property: PROP-W0-EIP7939-PRECOND-001. Type: Pre-condition. Primary element: ACTION-CHECK-GAS.

checklist_item.title: Falsify Invariant on STATE-EIP-SCHEDULED: All client implementations must agree on the same set of EIPs included

reachability.notes: Disagreement on included EIPs would cause consensus failures between different client implementations.

checklist_item.detection_procedure:
1. Invariant: All client implementations must agree on the same set of EIPs included in Fusaka: 11 core EIPs plus 4 supplementary, with no additions or omissions.
2. Enumerate all state transitions affecting 'STATE-EIP-SCHEDULED'.
3. Design test sequences to violate the invariant through:
   a. Rapid successive state transitions
   b. Boundary value inputs to state-changing operations
   c. Concurrent or re-entrant state modifications
4. Verify the invariant holds after each transition.
5. Check edge cases: empty state, maximum state, overflow.
6. Severity justification: Client disagreement on included EIPs causes network partition along client lines, potentially affecting >50% of the network.
executable_checks: Create state transition sequence targeting STATE-EIP-SCHEDULED invariant violation
Test boundary values for state transitions affecting STATE-EIP-SCHEDULED
Verify invariant holds under concurrent modifications
checklist_item.notes: Source property: PROP-W1-EIP7607-INV-003. Type: Invariant. Primary element: STATE-EIP-SCHEDULED.

reachability.notes: First validation barrier against malformed gossip data

checklist_item.detection_procedure:
1. Identify the trust boundary at EDGE-GOSSIP-RECEIVE (trust level: UNTRUSTED, target: unknown).
2. Property: Before accepting a data_column_sidecar from gossip (EDGE-GOSSIP-RECEIVE boundary), the node MUST run verify_data_column_sidecar to check: sidecar.index < NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS, len(kzg_commitments) > 0, len(kzg_commitments) <= max_blobs_per_block, and column/commitments/proofs length consistency. Struct
3. Target interface: N/A.
4. Attack vectors: generic boundary fuzzing.
5. Craft adversarial inputs crossing this boundary to test validation completeness.
6. Verify all input validation, authentication, and data sanitization are enforced.
7. Check for bypass paths that skip validation.
8. Severity justification: Malformed structural data could crash parsers or cause memory issues if not validated. Affects all gossip-receiving nodes but peer penalties mitigate spread.

reachability.notes: Ensures served data maintains gossip validation guarantees

checklist_item.detection_procedure:

2. Property context: Before responding to DataColumnSidecarsByRange or ByRoot, the node MUST ensure only sidecars that pass gossip validation rules are sent. Never send sidecars from blocks failing gossip or state transit
3. Verify that ACTION-VERIFY-GOSSIP-VALIDITY-RESPONSE correctly validates/processes data that has crossed the trust boundary.
4. Check internal invariants are maintained even if boundary validation is partially bypassed.
5. Test with edge-case inputs that pass boundary validation but stress internal handling.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for EDGE-CALL-ARRIVES: ModExp precompile calldata must contain at least 96 bytes for the three length f

reachability.notes: Any EOA or contract can call ModExp with arbitrary calldata via on-chain transaction. Malformed input handling differences between clients could cause consensus splits.

checklist_item.detection_procedure:
1. Identify the trust boundary at EDGE-CALL-ARRIVES (trust level: UNTRUSTED, target: unknown).
2. Property: ModExp precompile calldata must contain at least 96 bytes for the three length fields (base_length, modulus_length, exponent_length). Short calldata must be zero-padded, not rejected.
3. Target interface: N/A.
4. Attack vectors: generic boundary fuzzing.
5. Craft adversarial inputs crossing this boundary to test validation completeness.
6. Verify all input validation, authentication, and data sanitization are enforced.
7. Check for bypass paths that skip validation.
8. Severity justification: Different clients handling malformed ModExp input differently would cause consensus split via a single crafted transaction.

checklist_item.title: Falsify Post-condition on STATE-RECONSTRUCTION-FAILED: When reconstruction fails due to insufficient columns, the node MUST c

reachability.notes: Attacker withholding columns could trigger this path; node must not use partial data.

checklist_item.detection_procedure:
1. Post-condition: When reconstruction fails due to insufficient columns, the node MUST correctly report data unavailability and NOT attempt to use partial data.
2. Execute the operation with various valid inputs.
3. After each execution, verify:
   a. All expected side-effects occurred correctly
   b. No unexpected state changes occurred
   c. Return values match the post-condition specification
4. Test with boundary inputs that are valid but extreme.
5. Check partial execution/failure scenarios.
6. Severity justification: Using partial reconstructed data as if complete could cause incorrect state computations.
executable_checks: Execute operation on STATE-RECONSTRUCTION-FAILED and verify all post-condition guarantees
Check for unexpected side-effects after operation completion
Test partial failure scenarios for post-condition violations
checklist_item.notes: Source property: PROP-W5-EIP7594-RECON-POSTCOND-002. Type: Post-condition. Primary element: STATE-RECONSTRUCTION-FAILED.

checklist_item.title: Falsify Pre-condition on ACTION-CHECK-TX-GAS-VS-BLOCK-LIMIT: If a per-transaction gas cap (EIP-7825) is active, the transaction gas

reachability.notes: Without per-tx cap enforcement, a single transaction could consume entire 60M gas block, enabling worst-case execution.

checklist_item.detection_procedure:
1. Pre-condition: If a per-transaction gas cap (EIP-7825) is active, the transaction gas limit MUST also be validated against that cap, independent of the block gas limit.
2. Enumerate inputs that should be rejected by this pre-condition.
3. Attempt bypass with:
   a. Invalid/malformed inputs that may slip through validation
   b. Edge case values (zero, max, negative, empty)
   c. Type confusion or encoding tricks
4. Verify correct rejection of all invalid inputs.
5. Check if bypassing leads to exploitable state.
6. Severity justification: Missing per-tx gas cap check could allow single transactions to monopolize block capacity, causing execution time spikes on >5% of nodes.
executable_checks: Attempt precondition bypass on ACTION-CHECK-TX-GAS-VS-BLOCK-LIMIT with crafted invalid inputs
Test edge-case values (zero, max, overflow) against precondition
Verify rejection behavior is consistent across all code paths

reachability.notes: Non-deterministic or incorrect lookahead computation causes proposer disagreements.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Execute initialize_proposer_lookahead on a Fulu post-fork state. 2. Verify the result contains exactly (MIN_SEED_LOOKAHEAD + 1) * SLOTS_PER_EPOCH entries. 3. Verify each entry is a valid validator index from the active validator set. 4. Verify no duplicate entries where the same validator appears in impossible positions. 5. Cross-client comparison of the full lookahead array.

checklist_item.title: Falsify PRIMITIVE_ROOT_OF_UNITY Validity as Subgroup Generator

reachability.notes: If PRIMITIVE_ROOT_OF_UNITY is wrong, all FFT operations are incorrect.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Verify PRIMITIVE_ROOT_OF_UNITY^(2^k) == 1 where 2^k is the required subgroup order. 2. Verify PRIMITIVE_ROOT_OF_UNITY^(2^(k-1)) != 1 (it's a generator, not a lower-order element). 3. Verify the constant is within the BLS12-381 scalar field (< BLS_MODULUS). 4. Cross-implementation comparison of the constant value. 5. Compute a simple FFT and verify correctness against a known test vector.
executable_checks: Verify PRIMITIVE_ROOT_OF_UNITY order equals required subgroup order
Verify it's a generator (not lower-order element)
Cross-implementation comparison of the constant
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W7-PCS-ASSUM-INV-001. Internal property (Invariant), Formal Verification mindset. CRITICAL: wrong root of unity breaks ALL cell encoding and KZG verification.

checklist_item.title: Verify rejection enforcement and side-effects after sidecar validation failure
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Craft DataColumnSidecars that fail each validation type (format/inclusion/KZG). 2. Submit via P2P. 3. Verify state transitions: check sidecar is NOT stored in database. 4. Verify sidecar is NOT forwarded to other peers. 5. Check peer reputation/penalty is applied. 6. Attempt to retrieve rejected sidecar from storage. 7. Monitor network traffic for forwarding attempts. 8. Verify error logs indicate rejection reason.
executable_checks: Submit malformed sidecar (invalid format) and verify storage query returns null
Submit sidecar with inclusion proof failure and verify no gossip forwarding occurs
Submit sidecar with KZG proof failure and verify peer score is decreased
After rejection, query database for sidecar hash and confirm not found
Monitor P2P message queue to ensure rejected sidecar is not relayed
Check that STATE-SIDECAR-REJECTED is reached via EDGE-VALIDATION-FAIL-FORMAT
Check that STATE-SIDECAR-REJECTED is reached via EDGE-VALIDATION-FAIL-INCLUSION
Check that STATE-SIDECAR-REJECTED is reached via EDGE-VALIDATION-FAIL-KZG
Verify unexpected state changes: no transition to accepted/stored states
Confirm penalty mechanism triggers for sending peer
checklist_item.notes: Traced from PROP-W1-DASCORE-POSTCOND-001 covering nodes=[STATE-SIDECAR-REJECTED], edges=[EDGE-VALIDATION-FAIL-FORMAT, EDGE-VALIDATION-FAIL-INCLUSION, EDGE-VALIDATION-FAIL-KZG]

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for EDGE-FINALIZED-IGNORE: Finalized slot rejection enforcement
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Obtain current finalized checkpoint epoch from beacon state. 2. Compute start slot of finalized epoch. 3. Craft data_column_sidecar with block_header.slot equal to finalized slot. 4. Inject via gossip network. 5. Monitor for IGNORE action (no propagation, no storage, no penalty). 6. Repeat with slot < finalized slot. 7. Verify boundary: slot = finalized_slot + 1 should proceed to next validation rule. 8. Test with slot overflow and underflow values.
executable_checks: Send data_column_sidecar with slot equal to finalized epoch start slot and verify IGNORE
Send data_column_sidecar with slot less than finalized epoch start slot and verify IGNORE
Send data_column_sidecar with slot equal to (finalized epoch start slot + 1) and verify processing continues
Monitor that IGNOREd sidecars are not propagated to peers
Monitor that IGNOREd sidecars are not written to local DA storage
Verify no peer penalty is applied for sending finalized sidecars
Test slot value at uint64 boundary (0, max) with finalized checkpoint
checklist_item.notes: Traced from PROP-W2-FULU-GOSSIP-PRECOND-04 covering edges EDGE-TIMELY-PASS, EDGE-FINALIZED-IGNORE (boundary guard)

checklist_item.title: Bypass RANDAO Seed Availability Pre-condition for Proposer Lookahead
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Identify the proposer lookahead computation entry point. 2. Attempt to invoke lookahead computation for future epoch N+k where k > MIN_SEED_LOOKAHEAD. 3. Deliberately use beacon state where epoch N randao seed is unavailable. 4. Test with unfinalized recent epoch seed. 5. Test with future epoch beyond available randao_mixes buffer. 6. Test with corrupted or zero-valued seed. 7. Monitor if computation proceeds despite missing/invalid seed.
executable_checks: Call compute_proposer_lookahead for epoch E+10 when current finalized epoch is E, verify rejection
Provide beacon state with randao_mixes[target_epoch] = 0x0 (uninitialized), attempt lookahead computation, verify failure
Request lookahead for epoch beyond EPOCHS_PER_HISTORICAL_VECTOR, confirm unavailable seed error
Invoke lookahead during non-finalized epoch transition, verify seed finalization check enforced
Corrupt randao seed for target epoch in state, verify lookahead computation detects invalidity
Test with justification/finalization lag: seed exists but not finalized, verify pre-condition enforced
Simulate state with missing historical randao entry, confirm graceful error instead of panic
checklist_item.notes: Traced from PROP-W3-FULU-BEACON-LOOKAHEAD-PRE-001 covering ACTION-GET-SEED, ACTION-PROCESS-PROPOSER-LOOKAHEAD

checklist_item.title: Falsify Invariant: Induce divergent ForkDigestValues across nodes for identical fork version
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Deploy 2+ nodes with identical genesis config and fork version. 2. Trigger ForkDigestValue computation on each node. 3. Extract computed digest from each node. 4. Compare digests byte-by-byte. 5. Analyze source of non-determinism if mismatch exists.
executable_checks: Multi-node test: compute ForkDigestValue for fork version 0x04000000 on 10 nodes, verify all produce identical digest
Endianness test: verify digest computation handles byte order consistently across architectures
Genesis config variation: test with different genesis_validators_root values, verify deterministic digest per root
Timestamp independence: compute digest at different system times, verify identical output
Uninitialized memory test: run computation with MSAN/Valgrind to detect non-deterministic reads
checklist_item.notes: Traced from PROP-W4-EIP7892-INV-004 covering ForkDigestValue computation across all nodes

checklist_item.title: Falsify Pre-condition: Bypass all-gas-consumption requirement by triggering partial gas refund on MODEXP violation
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Craft transaction with 100,000 gas allocated to MODEXP call. 2. Include input violation: length_of_BASE = 2000. 3. Submit transaction. 4. Monitor gas consumption. 5. Verify ALL 100,000 gas consumed (no refund). 6. Test multiple violation types.
executable_checks: Test length_of_BASE = 2000 with 100,000 gas: verify exactly 100,000 gas consumed
Test length_of_EXPONENT = 2000 with 50,000 gas: verify exactly 50,000 gas consumed
Test length_of_MODULUS = 2000 with 75,000 gas: verify exactly 75,000 gas consumed
Compare gas consumption: valid MODEXP call vs invalid call, verify invalid consumes all allocated gas
Code review: verify MODEXP violation triggers full gas consumption, not normal revert with refund
EVM opcode test: verify MODEXP uses correct revert mechanism that consumes all gas
Gas estimation attack: attempt to estimate gas for invalid MODEXP, verify estimation prevents DoS
checklist_item.notes: Traced from PROP-W5-EIP7823-GAS-PRECOND-001 covering MODEXP gas consumption on input violation

checklist_item.title: Falsify eth/69 Validation: Bypass Canonical Hash Check with Malicious Block Data
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Establish eth/69 P2P connection with malicious peer. 2. Send block data with incorrect hash, modified transactions, or invalid PoW field handling. 3. Verify if validation against canonical block hash occurs before state transition. 4. Attempt to inject blocks with absent PoW fields that cause parsing errors. 5. Confirm PoW field absence is correctly handled per EIP-7927 spec.
executable_checks: Send eth/69 block with hash not matching canonical chain and verify rejection
Provide block with modified transaction list but valid header and verify validation detects mismatch
Send block with present PoW fields (totalDifficulty, ommers) post-Merge and verify handling per spec
Omit required fields in eth/69 block and verify parsing error before validation
Flood sync with blocks failing canonical hash check and verify DoS resistance
checklist_item.notes: Traced from PROP-W6-EIP7927-NET-PRECOND-003 covering eth69_block_validation

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for EDGE-RECEIVE-BLOCK: parent_root validation on P2P blocks

reachability.notes: Attacker sends blocks with unknown parent roots to disrupt fork choice.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Connect a malicious peer to a target node. 2. Send a signed beacon block with a parent_root set to a random 32-byte hash not in the node's store.block_states. 3. Verify the block is REJECTED before any state transition is applied. 4. Confirm no partial state update occurs (e.g., no entry added to block_states). 5. Verify peer scoring penalty is applied.
executable_checks: P2P test: send block with random parent_root, assert rejection
State check: verify store.block_states unchanged after rejection
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0-FULUFC-PRECOND-001. Boundary edge: EDGE-RECEIVE-BLOCK (EDGE-P2P-BLOCK-TO-FORKCHOICE). Blocks with unknown parents must be rejected at the boundary.

checklist_item.title: Falsify custody group assignment determinism across implementations

reachability.notes: Non-deterministic custody assignment would cause nodes to disagree on data availability obligations, causing network splits.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Call get_custody_groups with identical (node_id, custody_group_count) across all client implementations. 2. Verify outputs are identical sets. 3. Test edge cases: node_id = 0, node_id = 2^256-1, custody_group_count = 1, custody_group_count = NUMBER_OF_CUSTODY_GROUPS. 4. Call the function 1000 times with the same inputs and verify all outputs are identical (detect non-determinism from uninitialized memory or hash randomization).
executable_checks: Cross-client test: same (node_id, count) produces same group set in all clients
Fuzz: random node_ids, verify deterministic output across repeated calls
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1-DASCORE-INV-001. Formal Verification mindset: attempting to find inputs causing divergent custody assignments.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for gossip data_column_sidecar: proposer signature verification

reachability.notes: Cryptographic authentication of block proposer

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Construct a data_column_sidecar with a valid structure but an invalid proposer signature on signed_block_header. 2. Gossip it to target node. 3. Verify REJECT with peer penalty. 4. Test: valid signature but wrong proposer key (not the expected proposer for that slot). 5. Test: signature over different data (replay from different slot).
executable_checks: P2P test: gossip sidecar with random signature, assert REJECT
P2P test: gossip sidecar with valid sig from wrong proposer, assert REJECT
P2P test: replay sidecar from different slot, assert REJECT
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2-FULU-GOSSIP-PRECOND-05. Boundary edge: EDGE-GOSSIP-DATA-COLUMN-SIDECAR. Rule 5 of gossip validation.

checklist_item.title: Falsify RANDAO mix finalization for proposer seed computation

reachability.notes: Validators can manipulate RANDAO reveals to bias proposer selection. MIN_SEED_LOOKAHEAD mitigates this but the window is small.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Verify that process_proposer_lookahead uses RANDAO mixes from a finalized epoch (before current epoch's RANDAO was revealed). 2. Verify the seed used is from epoch N - MIN_SEED_LOOKAHEAD - 1. 3. Attempt to falsify: check if a validator withholding their RANDAO reveal at epoch boundary can influence the next epoch's proposer schedule. 4. Test with EIP-7917 deterministic lookahead: verify the lookahead is computed using the correct historical RANDAO mix.
executable_checks: Test: verify get_seed uses RANDAO mix from correct historical epoch
Test: validator skips RANDAO reveal, verify proposer schedule unaffected for target epoch
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3-FULU-BEACON-RANDAO-INV-001. RANDAO manipulation is a known risk, but EIP-7917 changes impact by making assignments visible earlier.

checklist_item.title: Falsify MODEXP violation gas exhaustion post-condition

reachability.notes: Gas accounting post-violation must leave exactly zero gas in the call frame.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Call MODEXP with an oversized input (violation) from a call frame with known gas amount G. 2. After the precompile returns, verify remaining gas in the call frame is exactly 0. 3. Attempt to falsify: check if any gas remains (would allow post-violation computation to differ between clients). 4. Test: nested calls — outer frame calls MODEXP with violation, verify outer frame sees the inner frame exhausted.
executable_checks: Test: MODEXP violation with G gas, assert 0 remaining after call
Test: nested call with MODEXP violation, verify caller sees correct gas deduction
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W5-EIP7823-GAS-POSTCOND-001. Post-condition: exactly zero gas must remain.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for EDGE-BLOB-ENGINE-API: blob length validation before polynomial encoding

reachability.notes: Blob data from Engine API (semi-trusted) or network could have incorrect length. Missing validation could cause buffer overflows or incorrect polynomial encoding.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Provide a blob with BYTES_PER_BLOB - 1 bytes. Verify rejection before polynomial conversion. 2. Provide a blob with BYTES_PER_BLOB + 1 bytes. Verify rejection. 3. Provide a blob with exactly BYTES_PER_BLOB bytes. Verify acceptance. 4. Provide an empty blob (0 bytes). Verify rejection. 5. Test via mock Engine API: return malformed blob lengths.
executable_checks: Test: blob with BYTES_PER_BLOB - 1 bytes, assert rejection
Test: blob with BYTES_PER_BLOB + 1 bytes, assert rejection
Test: blob with BYTES_PER_BLOB bytes, assert acceptance
Test: empty blob, assert rejection
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W7-PCS-ENCODE-PRECOND-001. Boundary edge: EDGE-BLOB-ENGINE-API. Semi-trusted: EL could return malformed blobs.

checklist_item.title: Falsify active validator set filtering for proposer lookahead
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Create a beacon state with validators in various lifecycle stages: active, pending activation, exiting (exit_epoch == current_epoch + 1), exited, slashed. 2. Compute the proposer lookahead for the next epoch. 3. Verify that only validators with is_active_validator(validator, target_epoch) == True appear in the lookahead. 4. Edge case: validator whose exit_epoch == target_epoch (should still be active). 5. Edge case: validator whose activation_epoch == target_epoch (should be active). 6. Edge case: mass exit scenario where >50% of validators are exiting.
executable_checks: Verify exited validators (exit_epoch < target_epoch) never appear in lookahead
Verify pending validators (activation_epoch > target_epoch) never appear in lookahead
Test mass exit scenario: ensure no empty proposer slots
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0-EIP7917-INV-005. Invariant falsification. Related ambiguity: AMB-EIP7917-01. Including inactive validators in selection causes missed slots.

checklist_item.title: Falsify eth/69 Status message field correctness on handshake
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Initiate an eth/69 handshake from a test node to a target node. 2. Capture the Status message sent by the target. 3. Verify all fields are present and well-formed: version (must be 69), networkid, genesis hash, forkid, earliestBlock (new in eth/69, replaces TD), latestBlock, latestBlockHash. 4. Verify earliestBlock matches the node's actual earliest available block (post-history-expiry). 5. Edge case: node just started, has not yet synced — verify defaults are correct. 6. Verify the removed fields (TD, totalDifficulty) are NOT present.
executable_checks: Capture eth/69 Status message and verify all required fields present
Verify earliestBlock field is accurate
Verify totalDifficulty field is absent (removed in eth/69)
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2-EIP7642-HANDSHAKE-PRECOND-04. Pre-condition falsification. Malformed status messages cause peer isolation.

checklist_item.title: Verify dynamic blob commitment limit uses epoch-correct parameters
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Verify the blob commitment check calls get_blob_parameters with the block's epoch (get_current_epoch(state)), not a hardcoded value. 2. At a BLOB_SCHEDULE transition epoch, verify blocks at epoch N use limit_N and blocks at epoch N+1 use limit_N+1. 3. Verify the check is len(commitments) <= max, not len(commitments) < max.
executable_checks: Verify get_blob_parameters is called with block's epoch
Test boundary block at schedule transition epoch
Verify <= vs < comparison correctness
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3-FULU-EXEC-PAYLOAD-PRE-001. Supporting node check for boundary property. Ensures internal logic correctly computes the dynamic limit.

checklist_item.title: Verify column index validation occurs before any array indexing or storage
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Audit the sample processing code path: verify column_index bounds check is the first validation step, before any array access using the index. 2. Check for TOCTOU: ensure the index is not modified between validation and use. 3. Verify the bounds check uses NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS from the current configuration, not a hardcoded value.
executable_checks: Code audit: verify bounds check precedes array access
Verify NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS is not hardcoded but derived from config
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W5-EIP7594-DAS-PRECOND-002. Supporting node check. Ensures validation ordering prevents exploitation.

checklist_item.title: Falsify fork upgrade application during state replay at FULU_FORK_EPOCH boundary
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Start a node from a pre-fork checkpoint state. 2. Replay blocks through the FULU_FORK_EPOCH boundary. 3. Verify upgrade_to_fulu is called at exactly the correct slot (FULU_FORK_EPOCH * SLOTS_PER_EPOCH). 4. Verify the upgrade is applied regardless of syncing mode (full sync, checkpoint sync, optimistic sync). 5. Compare post-upgrade state root with a reference node that was live during the fork. 6. Test: skip the fork epoch slot entirely (empty slot). Verify the upgrade still applies at the epoch boundary.
executable_checks: Replay through fork epoch, verify upgrade_to_fulu is called at correct slot
Test with empty slot at fork epoch, verify upgrade still applies
Compare post-upgrade state root with reference
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W6-FULU-FORK-ST-PRECOND-002. Pre-condition falsification. Related ambiguity: AMB-FULU-FORK-01. Nodes that skip the fork during sync would fork off the main chain.

checklist_item.title: Falsify Prague-Electra simultaneous activation at the same slot/block boundary
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Verify the EL Prague activation timestamp and CL Electra activation epoch map to the same slot. 2. Compute: expected_el_timestamp = GENESIS_TIME + (ELECTRA_FORK_EPOCH * SLOTS_PER_EPOCH * SECONDS_PER_SLOT). 3. Verify the EL client's Prague activation timestamp matches this value. 4. Test: process a block at the activation boundary. Verify both EL and CL transitions apply to the same block. 5. Test: what happens if there's a missed slot at the activation boundary? Verify both layers still activate correctly. 6. Cross-check across all client pairs (EL+CL combinations).
executable_checks: Verify EL Prague timestamp matches CL Electra epoch * SECONDS_PER_SLOT + GENESIS_TIME
Process block at activation boundary, verify both layers transition
Test missed slot at boundary, verify activation still occurs
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W7-EIP7600-FORK-INV-002. Invariant falsification. Related ambiguity: AMB-EIP7600-02. EL/CL fork activation mismatch would split the entire network.

checklist_item.title: Falsify column mapping uniqueness: verify NUMBER_OF_CUSTODY_GROUPS * i + custody_group produces no overlaps
checklist_item.bug_class: Data Availability Failure / Incorrect Column Assignment
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. For all valid custody_group values [0, NUMBER_OF_CUSTODY_GROUPS) and all valid i values [0, columns_per_group): compute column_index = NUMBER_OF_CUSTODY_GROUPS * i + custody_group. 2. Collect all column indices into a set. Verify set size equals total expected columns (NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS). 3. Verify no duplicates exist. 4. Verify all column indices are in range [0, NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS). 5. Test with actual Fulu config values: NUMBER_OF_CUSTODY_GROUPS=128, NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS=128. 6. Verify each custody group maps to exactly the expected number of columns.
executable_checks: Assert all generated column indices are unique (set size == expected total)
Assert all column indices in [0, NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS)
Assert each custody group maps to exactly NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS / NUMBER_OF_CUSTODY_GROUPS columns
Assert complete coverage: union of all custody group columns == all columns
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1-DASCORE-INV-003. Invariant type. Critical: incorrect column mapping across all nodes would cause systematic data availability failures for the entire network.

checklist_item.title: Verify bloom filter recomputation from logs matches eth/68 format exactly
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Take a set of known receipts with their eth/68-format bloom filters. 2. Strip the bloom fields. Recompute blooms from the log entries. 3. Compare byte-for-byte: recomputed bloom must equal original. 4. Test with receipts containing 0 logs, 1 log, many logs. 5. Test with logs containing topics of various lengths. 6. Verify Keccak-256 hash function and bit-setting logic match the EIP-2 bloom filter specification exactly.
executable_checks: Assert recomputed bloom == original bloom for all test receipts
Assert bloom is correct for 0-log receipts (all zeros)
Assert bloom bit-setting follows EIP-2 specification exactly
Assert determinism: same logs always produce same bloom
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2-EIP7642-RECEIPT-POSTCOND-01. Post-condition type. Related assumption ASSUM-EIP7642-03. Incorrect bloom recomputation could cause receipt query failures affecting dApps.

checklist_item.title: Falsify reserve price condition: test for integer overflow in BLOB_BASE_COST * base_fee multiplication
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Compute BLOB_BASE_COST (2^13 = 8192) * base_fee_per_gas for extreme base_fee values. 2. Test with base_fee = 2^245 (maximum realistic): verify 2^13 * 2^245 = 2^258 doesn't overflow uint256. 3. Compare against GAS_PER_BLOB * get_blob_basefee(parent): test same extreme values. 4. Verify the comparison uses the correct operator: > (not >=). 5. Cross-check: implement the condition in multiple languages (Python, Go, Rust) and verify identical results for edge cases. 6. Related ambiguity AMB-EIP7918-01: verify the mode selection is correct.
executable_checks: Assert no uint256 overflow for max realistic base_fee values
Assert comparison operator is strictly greater-than
Assert mode selection matches spec for boundary values
Assert identical results across Python reference and client implementations
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3-EIP7918-RESERVE-PRE-001. Pre-condition type. Related ambiguity AMB-EIP7918-01. BLOB_BASE_COST * base_fee multiplication could overflow in some implementations. Wrong mode selection affects all blocks.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for custody assignment: node ID manipulation resistance
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Analyze the custody group assignment formula: compute_custody_groups(node_id, custody_group_count). 2. Attempt to find node_id values that concentrate custody on specific target columns. 3. Calculate the computational cost of generating a node_id that custodies a specific set of columns. 4. Verify node_id is derived from a key that is expensive to generate (e.g., requires PoW or staking). 5. Test distribution: generate 10000 random node_ids and verify custody assignments are approximately uniform across groups.
executable_checks: Assert custody group distribution is approximately uniform for random node IDs
Assert computational cost of targeting specific groups is prohibitive
Assert node_id derivation includes anti-grinding measures
Assert no easily-discoverable node_id patterns for custody concentration
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W5-EIP7594-DAS-ASSUM-001. Boundary property (Invariant type). Related assumption ASSUM-EIP7594-02. Custody concentration attack could allow targeted data withholding affecting >33% of network's DA guarantees.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for EDGE-PROPOSER-SELECTED: reject blocks from non-assigned proposers
checklist_item.bug_class: Unauthorized Block Injection / Fork Choice Manipulation
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Compute the proposer_lookahead for the current epoch. 2. Have a validator NOT assigned to slot S create a signed block for slot S. 3. Send via P2P gossip. Verify the block is REJECTED because proposer_index doesn't match lookahead[S]. 4. Verify the check uses the deterministic proposer_lookahead array (EIP-7917), not a recomputed value. 5. Test with a valid signature from the wrong proposer: the signature must be valid for the proposer's key, but the proposer must not be assigned to the slot. 6. Verify this check occurs before state transition.
executable_checks: Assert block from non-assigned proposer is rejected
Assert proposer verification uses deterministic lookahead array
Assert valid signature from wrong proposer still rejected
Assert check occurs before any state transition
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W6-BOUNDARY-MALICIOUS-VALIDATOR-001. Boundary property. Trust model edge: proposer must match lookahead. Related assumption ASSUM-FULU-FORK-02. Accepting unauthorized blocks could cause fork choice manipulation affecting >33% of network.

checklist_item.title: Verify all 4 Prague EL EIPs activate simultaneously at fork timestamp
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Process blocks up to the Prague fork timestamp. 2. At the first post-Prague block, verify all 4 EIPs are active: (a) EIP-2537 (BLS precompile): call the BLS12-381 precompile, verify it works. (b) EIP-2935 (BLOCKHASH): verify the system contract is deployed and functional. (c) EIP-7623 (calldata cost increase): verify increased calldata cost applies. (d) EIP-7702 (delegation): verify SetCode transactions are accepted. 3. Verify that pre-Prague none of these are active. 4. Cross-check: ensure no client activates these EIPs at different blocks.
executable_checks: Assert BLS precompile active at first post-Prague block
Assert BLOCKHASH system contract active at first post-Prague block
Assert increased calldata cost at first post-Prague block
Assert SetCode transactions accepted at first post-Prague block
Assert none active at last pre-Prague block
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W7-EIP7600-EL-POSTCOND-001. Post-condition type. Related assumption ASSUM-EIP7600-01. Partial EIP activation in some clients causes consensus disagreements affecting >33% of the network.

checklist_item.title: Verify post-boundary handling at STATE-PRECOMPILE-CALL-RECEIVED

reachability.notes: Standard EVM entry point; precompile is reachable from any transaction or internal call.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. After data crosses boundary at EDGE-CONTRACT-CALLS-PRECOMPILE, verify internal handling at STATE-PRECOMPILE-CALL-RECEIVED. 2. Check that validated data is correctly processed: Transactions calling the secp256r1 precompile at address 0x100 must have been validated through the standard transaction validation pipeline (signature, nonce, gas, balance) before precompile execution. 3. Verify no TOCTOU between boundary validation and internal use. 4. Confirm state transitions are correct post-validation.
executable_checks: Verify correct state transition at STATE-PRECOMPILE-CALL-RECEIVED after boundary crossing
Check for TOCTOU vulnerabilities between validation and use
Confirm no data corruption in internal processing path
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0-EIP7951-PRECOND-007. Supporting boundary check. Nodes under test: ['STATE-PRECOMPILE-CALL-RECEIVED'].

checklist_item.title: Verify post-boundary handling at STATE-ATTACKER-CRAFTED-BLOCK

reachability.notes: If size check occurs after expensive operations, attacker can cause resource exhaustion with a single oversized block.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. After data crosses boundary at EDGE-ATTACKER-SENDS, verify internal handling at STATE-ATTACKER-CRAFTED-BLOCK. 2. Check that validated data is correctly processed: An attacker-crafted oversized block must be rejected by the early size check before any expensive processing (signature verification, state execution) is performed. 3. Verify no TOCTOU between boundary validation and internal use. 4. Confirm state transitions are correct post-validation.
executable_checks: Verify correct state transition at STATE-ATTACKER-CRAFTED-BLOCK after boundary crossing
Check for TOCTOU vulnerabilities between validation and use
Confirm no data corruption in internal processing path
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1-EIP7934-PRECOND-003. Supporting boundary check. Nodes under test: ['STATE-ATTACKER-CRAFTED-BLOCK', 'ACTION-RECEIVE-MALICIOUS-BLOCK', 'ACTION-EARLY-SIZE-CHECK', 'STATE-ATTACK-MITIGATED'].

checklist_item.title: Verify post-boundary handling at ACTION-VERIFY-INCLUSION-PROOF

reachability.notes: Critical cryptographic binding of commitments to block

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. After data crosses boundary at EDGE-FINALIZED-ANCESTOR-OK, verify internal handling at ACTION-VERIFY-INCLUSION-PROOF. 2. Check that validated data is correctly processed: Before accepting a data_column_sidecar, the node MUST verify verify_data_column_sidecar_inclusion_proof: Merkle branch verification that kzg_commitments are included in block body_root at depth 4 (rule 10). Invalid proofs MUST result in REJECT. 3. Verify no TOCTOU between boundary validation and internal use. 4. Confirm state transitions are correct post-validation.
executable_checks: Verify correct state transition at ACTION-VERIFY-INCLUSION-PROOF after boundary crossing
Check for TOCTOU vulnerabilities between validation and use
Confirm no data corruption in internal processing path
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2-FULU-GOSSIP-PRECOND-08. Supporting boundary check. Nodes under test: ['ACTION-VERIFY-INCLUSION-PROOF', 'STATE-DATA-COLUMN-SIDECAR-REJECTED'].

checklist_item.title: Verify postcondition: After execution payload processing, if blob commitment count exceeds the dynamic limit, the block must be in STATE-PAYLO

reachability.notes: Correct rejection of over-limit blocks is critical for consensus.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Execute the operation at ACTION-CHECK-BLOB-COMMITMENTS-LIMIT. 2. Verify the postcondition: After execution payload processing, if blob commitment count exceeds the dynamic limit, the block must be in STATE-PAYLOAD-REJECTED. If within limit and EL verification passes, block must be in STATE-PAYLOAD-VALID. 3. Check for unexpected side effects or state changes. 4. Test with edge-case inputs that stress the postcondition. 5. Cross-client comparison of output states.
executable_checks: Execute operation at ACTION-CHECK-BLOB-COMMITMENTS-LIMIT with various inputs
Verify postcondition holds for all test cases
Check for unexpected side effects
Cross-client output comparison
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3-FULU-EXEC-PAYLOAD-POST-001. Mindset: Formal Verification Engineer. Type: Post-condition. Primary: ACTION-CHECK-BLOB-COMMITMENTS-LIMIT. Nodes: ['ACTION-CHECK-BLOB-COMMITMENTS-LIMIT', 'STATE-PAYLOAD-VALID', 'STATE-PAYLOAD-REJECTED']. Edges: ['EDGE-ALL-VALID', 'EDGE-BLOB-LIMIT-EXCEEDED'].

checklist_item.title: Verify postcondition: After successful reconstruction, the reconstructed data matrix MUST be complete (all columns present) and consistent wit

reachability.notes: Reconstructed data should be re-verified against commitments to ensure correctness.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Execute the operation at STATE-DATA-RECONSTRUCTED. 2. Verify the postcondition: After successful reconstruction, the reconstructed data matrix MUST be complete (all columns present) and consistent with the original KZG commitments. 3. Check for unexpected side effects or state changes. 4. Test with edge-case inputs that stress the postcondition. 5. Cross-client comparison of output states.
executable_checks: Execute operation at STATE-DATA-RECONSTRUCTED with various inputs
Verify postcondition holds for all test cases
Check for unexpected side effects
Cross-client output comparison
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W5-EIP7594-RECON-POSTCOND-001. Mindset: Formal Verification Engineer. Type: Post-condition. Primary: STATE-DATA-RECONSTRUCTED. Nodes: ['STATE-DATA-RECONSTRUCTED']. Edges: ['EDGE-RECONSTRUCTED'].

checklist_item.title: Verify postcondition: After history expiry, the sync algorithm MUST only sync from the merge block forward. Sync MUST NOT attempt to retrieve

reachability.notes: If sync still attempts pre-merge block retrieval after expiry, it could stall or fail.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Execute the operation at ACTION-ADJUST-SYNC-ALGORITHM. 2. Verify the postcondition: After history expiry, the sync algorithm MUST only sync from the merge block forward. Sync MUST NOT attempt to retrieve or validate pre-merge blocks from P2P peers. 3. Check for unexpected side effects or state changes. 4. Test with edge-case inputs that stress the postcondition. 5. Cross-client comparison of output states.
executable_checks: Execute operation at ACTION-ADJUST-SYNC-ALGORITHM with various inputs
Verify postcondition holds for all test cases
Check for unexpected side effects
Cross-client output comparison
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W6-EIP7927-POSTCOND-001. Mindset: Formal Verification Engineer. Type: Post-condition. Primary: ACTION-ADJUST-SYNC-ALGORITHM. Nodes: ['STATE-HISTORY-EXPIRED', 'ACTION-ADJUST-SYNC-ALGORITHM', 'STATE-POST-MERGE-SYNC-ONLY']. Edges: ['EDGE-ADJUST-SYNC', 'EDGE-SYNC-ADJUSTED'].

checklist_item.title: Verify post-boundary handling at STATE-PARTIAL-CELLS-RECEIVED

reachability.notes: Cells collected from P2P peers may be adversarially chosen. Missing recovery input validation could cause incorrect polynomial reconstruction.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. After data crosses boundary at EDGE-PARTIAL-INPUT, verify internal handling at STATE-PARTIAL-CELLS-RECEIVED. 2. Check that validated data is correctly processed: Recovery inputs MUST satisfy: len(cell_indices) == len(cells), count >= CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB/2, no duplicate indices, ascending order, all indices < CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB, each cell length == BYTES_PER_CELL. 3. Verify no TOCTOU between boundary validation and internal use. 4. Confirm state transitions are correct post-validation.
executable_checks: Verify correct state transition at STATE-PARTIAL-CELLS-RECEIVED after boundary crossing
Check for TOCTOU vulnerabilities between validation and use
Confirm no data corruption in internal processing path
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W7-PCS-RECOVER-PRECOND-001. Supporting boundary check. Nodes under test: ['STATE-PARTIAL-CELLS-RECEIVED', 'ACTION-VALIDATE-RECOVERY-INPUTS'].
